function_calls.result: {"query":"Ara IDE homepage","results":[{"title":"Ara-Ide - LinkedIn Nigeria","url":"https://ng.linkedin.com/company/ara-ide","snippet":"We are a fabric and leather works enterprise into production of unique indigenous handmade bags, shoes, and trainings. Website: http://www.ara-ide.com/."},{"title":"Ara AI — the self-driving IDE for product development","url":"https://ara.so","snippet":"Ara is the self-driving IDE for product development. It thinks ahead, runs agents, uses memory and skills, and keeps software moving without constant"},{"title":"Ara: Ara is the self-driving IDE for software teams. | Y Combinator","url":"https://www.ycombinator.com/companies/ara","snippet":"Ara is the self-driving IDE for software teams. It watches work in progress, suggests the next step, and executes tasks through non-invasive computer use, background agents, memory, and reusable skills. co-founder & cto @ Ara (P26) - With a background in EECS from UC Berkeley and scaling ML infra in industry, some notable milestones include having chaired Norway's largest AI student org, winning 1st place in the Norwegian AI Championship and running ultras (100km>). Ara - The World's First Self-driving IDE. Hey everyone, we’re Adi and Sven - cofounders of Ara 👋. **tl;dr:** we built an IDE for the future. Now what's left is planning the next step, and every IDE still waits. Adi worked in applied ML, at Scale AI and previously built an IDE company. Sven was an ML engineer on production systems and chaired Norway's largest student AI org. The goal is an IDE that runs the development loop end to end, not one that waits for instructions."},{"title":"Shop","url":"https://araide.shop/shop","snippet":"... Animal Skin: A Must-Have Accessory. Accessories ₦50,000.00. Our amazing customers. Home. About Us. Shop. Collection. Event. Ara Ide @ 2024. function_calls.name: fetch_page
function_calls.result:
{"url":"https://ara.so/","title":"Ara AI — the self-driving IDE for product development","text_excerpt":"Announcing Ara's backing from Y Combinator.
Ara
Product
FAQ
Changelog
Resources
Get Started
The self-driving IDE

Ara keeps product development moving without constant prompts.

Download for macOS
Windows waitlist

Trusted every day by builders at world-class teams

Stanford
MIT
UC Berkeley
Harvard
Airbnb
Meta
Ara turns product work into agents that keep moving.

The IDE should not sit still until someone writes another prompt. Ara can turn useful work into scheduled agents, background workflows, and recurring product-development loops.

Skills make the development loop self-improving.

When Ara learns a workflow, it can preserve the method as a skill so the next run starts with more context, sharper instructions, and less manual steering.

Memory, plugins, and computer use close the loop.

Hermes Cron, Hermes Skills, Memory, and Mac computer use give Ara the infrastructure to understand the codebase, act in local tools, remember the result, and prepare the next step.

MISSION CONTROL

True mission control for the token-maxxers.

Keep dense agent work visible across chats, files, browsers, terminals, and review panes while Ara keeps the loop moving.

See agent infrastructure
→
